---
title: "Machine Learning in Medical Sciences"
subtitle: "When Algorithms Meet Clinical Data"
description: |
  A gentle introduction to machine learning concepts for clinicians: supervised vs
  unsupervised learning, classification and regression, model validation,
  and real-world applications in diagnosis, prognosis, and drug discovery.
  No programming prerequisite — focuses on intuition and clinical interpretation.
categories:
  - Machine Learning
  - Artificial Intelligence
  - Clinical Prediction
  - Data Science
---

```{r}
#| label: setup
#| include: false

library(tidyverse)
library(kableExtra)
source("../R/mcq.R")
library(patchwork)
library(scales)

set.seed(42)

theme_clean <- function() {
  theme_minimal(base_size = 12) +
  theme(
    plot.title = element_text(face = "bold", size = 13),
    plot.subtitle = element_text(size = 11, color = "gray40"),
    panel.grid.minor = element_blank(),
    panel.grid.major = element_line(color = "gray90", linewidth = 0.3),
    axis.text = element_text(size = 10),
    axis.title = element_text(size = 11, face = "bold"),
    legend.position = "bottom",
    legend.title = element_text(face = "bold")
  )
}
```

::: {.callout-note appearance="minimal"}
**Lecture slides for this module:** [Open Slides](../slides/15-ml-slides.html){target="_blank"}
:::

## Learning Objectives

By the end of this module, you will be able to:

1. Distinguish between **supervised and unsupervised** learning
2. Understand common algorithms: **logistic regression, decision trees, random forests, neural networks**
3. Explain the **bias-variance trade-off** and why overfitting is dangerous
4. Interpret **model validation** metrics (accuracy, AUC, sensitivity, specificity — connecting back to Module 5)
5. Understand **training, validation, and test sets** — why splitting data matters
6. Evaluate ML claims in medical literature critically
7. Recognise **limitations and ethical concerns** of ML in healthcare
8. Identify promising **Indian applications** of ML in medicine

---

:::{.clinical-hook}
## Coming Soon

This module is under development. It will provide a conceptual introduction to machine learning for MBBS students — no coding required, but with R demonstrations for those interested.

**Planned content includes:**

- What is machine learning? (Pattern recognition from data)
- Supervised learning: classification (disease/no disease) and regression (predicting BP)
- From logistic regression (Module 11) to decision trees to random forests
- Neural networks: the basic intuition (without the math)
- The critical problem: overfitting — why a model can be perfect on training data and useless on new patients
- Cross-validation and the train/test split
- Connecting to Module 5: AUC, ROC curves, sensitivity/specificity as ML evaluation metrics
- Real Indian applications: retinal screening for diabetic retinopathy (Google/Aravind), TB detection from chest X-rays, ECG analysis for arrhythmia
- Ethical concerns: algorithmic bias, black-box decisions, data privacy
- The future: AI as clinical decision support, not replacement
- Indian clinical examples throughout
:::

---

## References

::: {#refs}
:::
